reasoning: Without current macroeconomic data for late 2025 (since web search tools are unavailable), the most reliable approach is applying the historical base rate. Over the last 35 years (approx. 275 scheduled FOMC meetings), the Fed has cut rates by 50+ basis points at roughly 14 scheduled meetings, notably during the 2001 bursting of the dot-com bubble, the 2007-2008 financial crisis, the COVID-19 pandemic, and the September 2024 easing cycle onset. This yields an unconditional base rate of approximately 5%. I set my probability at 6% to reflect this base rate while reasonably accounting for a very slight prediction market longshot bias.
